description: Driver-ed terminology glossary applied to source text before translation. Expansions happen client-side before the source goes to the vendor; preservations are wrapped in DeepL/Google glossary syntax (or instructed in a Claude system prompt) so the vendor renders them verbatim.

expansions.ANSTSE: American Novice Teen Driver Education Standards
expansions.ADTSEA: American Driver and Traffic Safety Education Association

register_notes.right-of-way: Translate as a legal-driving concept, not literally as 'right of way'. Use the established traffic-law term in the target language (e.g. 'prioridad de paso' in Spanish, 'droit de passage' in French).
register_notes.permit-eligible: Translate as 'qualified for a learner's permit' or the equivalent local-DMV phrasing — not literal.
register_notes.yield sign: Translate as the traffic-law term (e.g. 'señal de ceda el paso' in Spanish), not literal.
register_notes.rolling stop: Translate as 'incomplete stop' or 'failure to stop' in the target language's legal register.

audience: US teen drivers and their parents/guardians. Conversational register. Keep state-specific agency names verbatim; explain them parenthetically on first use if needed.
